perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video starts with a split-screen view; the left side shows a person's hand holding a clear bag of four large Taiwanese macarons (Fu-fei), while the right side shows a young woman with long dark hair and a pink shirt looking curious. (2) The woman is then seen outdoors wearing a white shirt, walking past a bakery shelf and making a phone call on her smartphone with a frustrated facial expression. (3) Back indoors, the woman uses a Shopee app on her phone and then reveals a large black Kangol brand bag containing socks and a box of macarons. (4) She opens the 'FLUFFY' macaron box, picks up a beige macaron, and takes a large bite, her eyes widening in a delighted and surprised expression. (5) The final sequence features rapid cuts of promotional graphics for the Shopee 517 Foodie Festival, highlighting various snacks and a Kangol bag.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) High-tempo, upbeat electronic pop music starts immediately and remains consistent throughout the video. (2) A female voice speaks in an energetic, high-pitched tone in Chinese, narrating her experience. (3) During the phone call scene, sound effects of a phone ringing and a filtered voice representing the person on the other end are heard. (4) As the woman eats, exaggerated 'crunch' and 'munch' sound effects are inserted, followed by a 'ping' sound effect when she shows a thumbs-up gesture. (5) The music volume increases slightly at the end during the promotional call to action.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Hook Text: "只有台灣吃得到? 第一次吃台式馬卡龍😂" (Only available in Taiwan? First time eating Taiwanese macarons). (2) Functional Text: Subtitles tracking the dialogue about searching for the dessert at Taipei Main Station and mentions of "阿公 阿嬤" (Grandpa and Grandma). (3) Decorative Text: "實驗歐膩" (Unnie Lab) logo appearing on screen. (4) Promotional Text: "蝦皮 x 萬歲牌 x 小熊菓子 限量聯名款" (Shopee x Viva x Bear Bakery limited edition co-brand) and "5/13-6/18 獨家在蝦皮上架" (Exclusive on Shopee).

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro/Hook -> Offline Search -> App Discovery -> Unboxing/Tasting -> Promotional Offer

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main female creator is visible throughout the video.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young Asian woman with long, dark wavy hair, wearing a pink t-shirt in the studio and a white t-shirt while outdoors.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: The video alternates between a bright, clean indoor room with a gaming chair, a busy city street, and a display shelf in a bakery/store.
visual_scene_and_style.lightingStyle: Bright, even studio lighting with natural daylight in outdoor scenes.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: Montage: Hook -> Conflict -> Resolution -> Consumption -> Promotional CTA
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video is a promotional advertisement for the Shopee 517 Foodie Festival. It uses a narrative where the creator attempts to find 'Taiwanese macarons' offline, encounters difficulty, and ultimately finds a limited-edition co-branded set (Shopee x Viva x Fluffy) on the Shopee app. The content combines a personal 'first-time trial' vlog style with explicit product endorsements to drive traffic to the Shopee platform.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Direct presentation of product packaging, demonstration of app usage, and specific discount dates/codes displayed on screen.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Usage of the 'Unnie Lab' (實驗歐膩) branding and a focus on cross-cultural food experiences (Korean in Taiwan).
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video effectively utilizes 'FOMO' (fear of missing out) by emphasizing the 'limited edition' nature of the product and the difficulty of finding it in physical stores.
